w:r.w:t: The College Club Management System (CCMS) is a full-stack web application designed to centralize the management of student clubs and extracurricular activities within a college ecosystem. The platform serves as a single digital hub where students, club administrators, and college faculty can interact, coordinate events, manage memberships, and communicate effectively.

Most colleges today manage their club activities through fragmented channels — paper forms, WhatsApp groups, and spreadsheets — leading to poor communication, missed events, and inefficient administration. This project aims to solve these pain points with a structured, technology-driven solution.
Build a responsive, multi-page web application for managing college clubs
Implement RESTful communication between frontend and backend using JSON
Use Jakarta Servlets on Apache Tomcat as the backend server layer
Use JDBC to connect the backend to a relational database (MySQL/PostgreSQL)
Apply CSS Box Model, Flexbox, and Grid layout techniques throughout the UI
Implement DOM Manipulation for dynamic user interactions
Demonstrate full CRUD operations across all major modules
The system covers the following major functional areas:
Student registration, login, and profile management
Role-based access control (Student, Club Admin, Super Admin)
Spacing, padding, borders for cards, modals, forms
Navigation bar, card rows, form alignment, footers
Dashboard grids, club listing pages, event calendars
Dynamic content, form validation, JSON fetch, UI updates
Async requests to Servlet endpoints, JSON parsing
Handles HTTP GET/POST requests, processes business logic
Serialization/deserialization of Java objects to JSON
Stores users, clubs, events, memberships, announcements
css/style.css — Global styles, box model, flexbox, grid
js/app.js — DOM manipulation, Fetch API calls, JSON handling
dao/ — Data Access Objects using JDBC
model/ — Java POJOs (User, Club, Event, Membership)
Register, login, browse clubs, join/leave clubs, register for events, view announcements
All student permissions + create/edit club info, post events, manage members, post announcements
Full system access — manage all clubs, users, events, approve/reject club creation requests
FR-01: Students can register with name, email, roll number, and password
FR-02: Registered users can log in; invalid credentials show an error via DOM
FR-03: Login state stored in sessionStorage; protected pages redirect if unauthenticated
FR-04: Users can update their profile (name, bio, profile picture)
FR-05: Password change via current password confirmation
FR-06: Students can browse all available clubs with search and filter functionality
FR-07: Club cards show name, category, member count, and a Join button
FR-08: Students can join or leave a club; membership reflected instantly via DOM update
FR-09: Club Admins can create a new club with name, description, category, and logo
FR-10: Club detail page shows description, members list, events, and announcements
FR-11: Super Admin approves/rejects new club creation requests
FR-12: Club Admins can create events with title, date, time, venue, and description
FR-13: Students can browse and register for upcoming events
FR-14: Events page displays a filterable grid of upcoming and past events
FR-15: Event detail page shows registration count, remaining seats, and countdown timer
FR-16: Students receive a JSON-based confirmation response on event registration
FR-17: Club Admins can post announcements with title, body, and priority tag
FR-18: Students see a live announcement feed on their dashboard
FR-19: Announcements are fetched dynamically via Fetch API and rendered with DOM manipulation
FR-20: Dashboard shows total clubs, members, events, and recent activity
FR-21: Super Admin can deactivate/activate clubs or users
FR-22: Club Admin dashboard shows member list with role management
FR-23: All dashboard data is fetched from Servlet endpoints as JSON
Pages should load within 2 seconds on localhost
Fully responsive layout using Flexbox and Grid for mobile/tablet/desktop
Server-side validation in Servlets; client-side via JS DOM
All DB queries use JDBC PreparedStatements — no raw string queries
All Servlet errors return a structured JSON error response
DAO pattern separates DB logic from Servlet logic
Compatible with Chrome, Firefox, and Edge modern versions
The database is designed around five core entities: Users, Clubs, Events, Memberships, and Announcements. JDBC PreparedStatements are used throughout for secure, parameterized queries.
All endpoints return JSON responses. Servlets are mapped via @WebServlet annotation on Tomcat 10+.
Club Cards: margin + padding + border + border-radius applied for card appearance
Forms: padding on inputs; margin-bottom for spacing; box-sizing: border-box globally
Modals: border + padding + box-shadow for overlay dialogs
Announcements: padding + color-coded left-border for priority levels
Navbar: display:flex with justify-content:space-between for logo + nav links
Club Card Row: flex-wrap:wrap with gap for responsive club listing
Login Form: flex column with align-items:center for centered form
Footer: flex with space-between for left info and right social links
Member list items: flex row with avatar + name + role badge
Dashboard Overview: 3-column grid for stats cards (clubs, members, events)
Club Listing Page: auto-fill responsive grid — grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(280px, 1fr))
Admin Panel: 2-column grid for sidebar + main content
Event Calendar View: 7-column grid representing days of the week
Dynamically render club cards after fetching JSON from /api/clubs
Show/hide login modal without page reload
Update member count on Join button click without refresh
Filter clubs in real-time by category using input event listener
Display validation error messages below form fields on submit
Append new announcements to the feed dynamically on dashboard load
Hero section (Flexbox), club category grid, navbar, CTA buttons
Centered form (Flex column), DOM validation messages, fetch on submit
Grid stats row, my clubs list, announcements feed (DOM rendered)
Responsive CSS Grid cards, search input, category filter tabs
Banner, flex member row, events tab, announcements tab
Event grid, countdown timers (DOM), register button + seat counter
Grid KPI cards, tables for members/events, approve/reject actions
Edit form, my clubs list, event history, avatar upload
DB schema finalized, Tomcat configured, project structure set
User registration/login Servlets + JDBC DAOs working
All Club/Event/Membership Servlet endpoints returning JSON
HTML pages with CSS Box Model, Flexbox, Grid layouts
JS integration: Fetch API calls, JSON rendering, DOM manipulation
Super Admin + Club Admin panels with CRUD
End-to-end testing, error handling, responsive testing
Complete WAR deployment on Tomcat, final documentation
JDBC connection failures on Tomcat startup
Use connection pooling with singleton DBUtil class, add null checks
JSON parsing errors in Servlet response
Wrap Gson.toJson() in try-catch and return error JSON on failure
Cross-Origin issues in Fetch API calls
Add CORS headers in Servlet doGet/doPost using response headers
Use JDBC PreparedStatement exclusively — never string-concat queries
Use HttpSession API in Servlets for login state tracking
Use media queries + fluid grid units; test on Chrome DevTools
HTML semantic structure with proper use of forms, tables, sections
CSS Box Model applied (margin, padding, border, sizing)
CSS Flexbox used for at least 3 layout areas
CSS Grid used for at least 2 layout areas
DOM Manipulation (dynamic rendering without page reload)
Jakarta Servlet handling GET and POST with JSON output
JDBC with PreparedStatement for all DB operations
"message": "Club not found or user already a member" }
Gson 2.10+ — Java library for JSON serialization/deserialization
MySQL Connector/J 8.x — JDBC driver for MySQL
Jakarta Servlet API 6.x — Servlet specification for Tomcat 10+
BCrypt (jBCrypt) — for hashing passwords before storing in DB
IDE: IntelliJ IDEA or Eclipse with Jakarta EE support
Browser Testing: Chrome DevTools for responsive layout and network inspection
API Testing: Postman or Bruno for testing Servlet endpoints
End of Document — College Club Management System PRD v1.0
